description: Apollo 16 Flight Journal - Day 9, Part 1: Preparation for LM Jettison

tokens: Apollo Control, Houston; 189 hours, 29 minutes [189:17] Ground Elapsed Time.
We're standing by now for a wakeup call to the crew of Apollo 16.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: How's your writing arm this morning?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Wait until he gets the sleep out of his eyes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The first thing I want to do, Hank, is to check on the lithium canister.
I got - I had some trouble getting that one out of - out of B last night; and let me go down and check that other one before I do anything else and make sure I don't get it stuck.
These things are swelling a little bit now, and they've been a little - a little difficult to get to.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's Ken Mattingly aboard Apollo 16 talking to CapCom Hank Hartsfield here in Mission Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Charlie's going off biomed here for a while.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ken really had a hard time getting that LiOH canister out last night.
He had to wiggle it back and forth about 20 times and go - and come forward and then he'd push it back, come forward, and push it back.
Finally got it worked out of there.
But what he was worried about and what - what worried me is, is there a possibility of busting that nylon strap on there off?
In which case you'd probably never get it out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think I remember breaking off one sometime a long time ago, and I just wondered if they'd beefed that thing up any.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I had a little trouble getting the one out - let's see, which one was it last night?
It was the - it was the B, and I had just a little trouble getting the one out yesterday morning, or whenever it was we made the other change of A.
But it was just a little sticky; it wasn't like this.
I think we may have had a little extra humidity because the windows are starting to fog up more often now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I killed the jet monitor, and I - I am ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The first change occurs at 189:46 in the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 189:46 [189:35], add "Image Motion, On; Mapping Camera, On," and you might make a note there to perform this before opening the Mapping Camera/Laser Altimeter cover.
What we're going to do here is to turn the camera on before we open the door and use the Gamma-Ray Boom as a reference.
They want to try to get an idea of what's happening on this extend/retract on the Mapping Camera; and, later on, we'll do the same thing.
We'll turn the camera on and retract it all the way with it running and close the door before we turn it off.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 189:51 [189:40], delete "Image Motion, On; and delete Mapping Camera, On, T-start." Change the "Image Motion, Incr ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, I'd better get my maneuver going here before we do anything, else.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Press on with that, and let me know when you're ready to go again.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Would you check my Logic Power, on, on 181, please?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I didn't think I got to them last night to turn them off.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: There's two guarded switches down there.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, are you on Vox this morning?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: While I'm getting this roll going, why don't you give me some more of that stuff?
The last thing I copied was the deletion at 189:50 [189:38] of the Image Motion and the Mapping Camera, On.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And the "Image Motion, Increase", change the "Barber pole" to "Barber pole plus one."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: On the next page, at 190:15 [190:03], P52 ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Before I leave that, Hank, do you have a camera stop time?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, would you give us Accept, please?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're going to send you a new state vector; and FAO advises that stop time will be read up with the updates.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I think - you - you got your roll attitude now?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Let me get that straightened out first.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hank, there's no way we're going to make it to this attitude on time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I guess that's agreeable with everybody.
But I assume that - we might just as well hold off on this camera extension and all that until daylight.
It looks like it's going to be dark or do you want to do that right now while we're ...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We want to - we want to do it now.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Do that, and get this attitude, and take whatever lighting happens to be out there?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Yeah, you got it all dirty.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: IMC is On; it's barber pole and - gray.
The Mapping Camera is coming On -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The Laser Altimeter door is coming open -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Mapping Camera is going to Extend -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: While I'm here, I'll get the Gamma Ray Shield, Off, and the Laser Altimeter, On.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Image Motion is set, and ready to copy some - some more.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The next thing occurs at 191 hours.
I guess you can get it right at the top of that page, "CMP, open LM hatch."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 191:05 [190:53], may have to use an arrow to get it in there, "Image Motion, Increase, Barber Pole, On."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And for planning, about 191:06 [190:54], we plan to start reading up the LM changes if we can get through these.
At 191:16 [191:04], from that MSFN up-link black, delete the LOPC target-load information there in the desired orient.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 191:16 [191:04], you'll have to use an arrow again ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: For planning purposes, what book should the LM guys have available to write in?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The LM Contingency Checklist and the LM Timeline.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And delete the update block at 191:18 [191:06].
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, and I'll - stand corrected on that update block.
We'll give you a TEI PAD there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 191:33 [191:21], delete everything down to orbital science photos.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Well, that one I can do.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And at 191:50 [191:38], here's where we're going to close that mapping camera up; I guess the best thing to do is just scratch through all that stuff there and write out to the side the order we want to get it in, which is as follows: "Laser Altimeter, Off; Mapping Camera, Retract; Mapping Camera/Laser Altimeter Cover, Closed - that's after it's retracted, of course - "Mapping Camera, Off." And I think you're at attitude now, Ken, if you want to start your P20.
It's not at attitude, stand corrected.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Yeah, it must have bumped a stick there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'm glad somebody is awake this morning.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: After the Mapping Camera Off, wait 22 seconds, Mapping Camera, Standby; IMC Off" They want to do a Verb 49 to the LM comm, deep space measurement attitude to arrive at 192:10 [191:58].
High-Gain angles for AOS acq: Pitch, minus 35; Yaw 235.
And along about 213:00, beg - Stand corrected - 191:55 [191:43], want to start transferring equipment from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Now, give me that time that you've got to be in attitude again, I didn't get that one.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And you didn't say anything about the X-Ray.
Do you want it to go to Standby?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We want to leave the X-Ray, On.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: So all the rest of that block up there, I delete, right?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And leave the alpha-particle cover open and all that.
I'll just delete all those guys, and let me read you what I have.

That's the "Laser Altimeter - Starting at 191:50 [191:38] we'll go "Laser Altimeter, Off; Mapping Camera/Laser Altimeter when it's in, the "Mapping Camera/Laser Altimeter Cover" is to "Close," the Mapping Camera" then comes "Off" and we go through a little ditty of "33 seconds, Standby; IMC, Off." While we're doing this, we can do a Verb 49 to the attitude 322, 115, 305; AOS High Gain angles will be Pitch, minus 35; Yaw, 235.
I need to be in attitude by 192:10 [191:58].
Starting about 191:55 [191:43], you want to start transfer of LM equipment.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Now, if you'll make a little note there, go to 214 hours, Page 323.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: 214 hours, that's on three hundred and - Page 323.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'm going to give you both times.
If you like, you can write in the - your clock times over these.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hey, Hank - Hank, I'm getting - I'm getting confused.
Would you give an overall picture, so that I can have some - some perspective of what we're doing.
It - it'd sure made it a lot easier.
I can copy this line by line, but I'll never - never keep it all straight here if -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay We're gonna do the items in the Flight Plan up to 192:00 [191:48], and then we're gonna go over to 214 hours in the Flight Plan and pick up these items in there - until we get over to about 216:15.
And then we're gonna jump back to 176:55, and pick up the LM jett procedure.
And when we get through with that, we're gonna jump back to...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Let me absorb what you're saying.
When you say you're gonna pick up, these things over here at Rev 72, I guess you're looking at all these visual targets and photo targets.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: All right, I was gonna say because we're in - we're on a groundtrack that isn't very compatible with some of those things.
Okay, so then you're gonna do that and then you're gonna go back and do the LM jettison, and then come back up to the subsat?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we're trying to work at a ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: ...clock update in here somewhere to get all this to look right.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: That'll be the trick of the year.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Can you do that with a clock sync?
You're even better than I thought you were.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: even knows what time it is now.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: change the - the - Don't tell us things like that.

Let me - all right, let me - let's - so we can make sure we're all talking in the same hours, I would like to do like we did the other day and - and correct the - the - times in the Flight Plan that are printed now to be the times that we'll really do it.
And then you talk to me in times that we'll really do it and forget what it says in the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That worked out real good with the rendezvous.
Okay, so now the pages I need to fix the times on are going to be - starting at Page 323.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You have to subtract 21 hours and 50 minutes from these times.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: (Laughter) Sim Sup [Simulator Supervisor - ie this is like a simulation] is having a field day.
This will take me a few minutes, but I'll - I'll get there.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'm considering resigning after this shift.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Just a second, Hank, let me - I'll - I'll get this page done, and then we can work on it, and I'll be right with you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, can we get a Shield, On, Gamma Ray?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We got - we got a lot of things going on at one time here.
I've got Page 323 times corrected now, if you want to - if you want to correct those - those events before I work on the next page?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: At 192:10 [191:58], delete the galactic photo sequence.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: In fact, delete everything in that page, and then, at 192:23, LM activation begins.
Actually, everything in the left hand column, I should have said, should be deleted.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: LM activation goes where all that other stuff is.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The next column, all the P20 stuff; and at "Acquire MSFN," the High Gain
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Now, what about this Skylab contamination at the bottom of the page?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Start over again now in the next column.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The MSFN angles are minus 35, yaw, 238.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Delete the orbital science prep - the configure camera, et cetera, and the orbital science.
At 192:55 [191:43] there, we're going to up-link to the LM - just for your information - a state vector liftoff time P23 - P30 load and P99.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I hope that's not a lift-off time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: At 193:04 [192:52], we're gonna give you the LM jettison PAD.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're gonna go back to the other - the other part of the book?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You'll copy that at - on Page 283.
That's at 176:15 in the original Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: If you pull it out, I think, you'll probably find more behind it there.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: On the next page, we need to change all those times again.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Let me see what time that was.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, we're - oh - let me - which battery?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: At - at 193:10 [192:58], Verb 49 manouver to LM jett PAD attitude.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Delete the shape target load there in the MSFN up-link
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Delete the target - the orbital science stuff there - delete the rest of the things in that column and at 193:23, begin suit donning.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, And we're all waiting with bated breath to find out what time youendsuit donning.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I guess just when the end is end.
I - Let's - let's look here at the next column; 193:40.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Delete down through "Mapping Camera Laser Altimeter Cover, Close."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Are you - are you sure we wouldn't do better starting with a clean page like we did the other day once.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I've been thinking the same thing.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I didn't mean to be facetious about that, Hank - because it seems like I'm doing more deleting than I'm writing, and it's getting so that - it's getting so confused, I may miss writing down - You know, when it comes time to execute, I may miss something.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, if we had it to do over, I think this is what we ought to do.
This thing was just handed to me a few minutes ago, and we're trying to straighten it out.
You want to continue with this, or do you want to try to salvage...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Let's press on to this sequence until we get to LM jett, and see how it works.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: At - at 193:46 [193:34], and I guess you're just going to have to write this in the left-hand column and point over there because that's what I had to do.
We want "Mass Spec, Ion Source, Off; Mass Spec Experiment to Standby."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hank, let me - let me regroup now.
I got everything deleted out of all that stuff that's on there down through "Mapping Camera Cover, Close." That leaves the alpha cover to be closed.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: "Experiment to Standby." And then there's a little caution, wait 5 minutes before retracting Mass Spec boom.
"Gamma Ray, Retract; X-Ray to Standby; and then at 193:51 [193:39], which is your five minutes, Mass Spec, Retract."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: So I've deleted all that stuff that's there, except for the Alpha cover Close.
At 193:46 [193:34], we'll take the Mass Spec Ion Source, Off, and Experiment to Standby.
The Gamma Ray boom gets retracted, the X-Ray goes to Standby, and after 5 minutes, I retract the mass spec boom, and the alpha-particle cover will come closed.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And then you'll do the - you'll do the purge line Heaters, On, configure for the dump, and the rest of the things there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And on the next page, at 194:10 [193:58] -
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And on the next page, at 194:10 [193:58] -
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This - This PGA donning, with things going on during it, bothers me a little bit.

We - this PGA donning in our present configuration is - is a three-man effort, even if one man's job is to get in the corner and stay there - stay out of the way; and I'd like for us to do the minimum activity while we're putting those suits on.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: If we can do it in the LM, I guess that will relieve a lot of the load here.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I guess we're just gonna have to do the best we can, Ken, and we'll try to help as much as we can from down here.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It's not so much a problem entirely, it's just keeping track of things, and you just - once a guy starts putting that suit on, he commands the rest of the volume in the spacecraft.
It's a - that A7LB is a real - real interesting operation.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: If we can use the LM or it, that will help out a great deal.
[According to the CM transcript "If we can do it in the LM, I guess that'll relieve a lot of the load here".]
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: the "P20; CMC Mode, Auto; GDC Align."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Do the purges and dumps, delete the P30 remark there...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: and, at 194:23 [193:11], we're going to close the hatches.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Did you say delete the dumps?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Do the dumps and the purge, delete the P52, and the alignment and the P30 stuff.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Let me write the times on this page.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: At 194:23 [194:11], we've closed the hatches, and you do the "Purge Line Heaters, Off," and terminate the waste water dump; and, at this point ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: we're going to jump in the Flight Plan.
I think that's as far as we ought to go on this rev.
We're about two minutes from LOS ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: but, at this point, you're going to jump back to 176: 55.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: If you'll tell me time bias to put on that page, I'll have that ready.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'll catch that the next rev, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Can you give me the time bias so we can - I can do that?
Update the times on the page?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We weren't planning on doing that section.
We were just going to do all those steps.
There's about 45 minutes of stuff in there getting ready for the jett before we do it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: How about the - if I'm standing by with a blank page and just let you read me a new time line.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'll have it ready for you, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The next Public Affairs Officer transcript is slightly out of sync, to fit in better with the technical transcript.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 190 hours, 15 minutes [190:03] Ground Elapsed Time.
We've just had Loss Of Signal with Apollo 16 passing behind the Moon now, on Revolution 59.
During this frontside pass, Capcom, Hank Hartsfield, passing up a lengthy list of Flight Plan changes and revisions to Command Module Pilot Ken Mattingly.
We're at 190 hours, 16 minutes [190:04]; this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 190 hours 58 minutes [190:46] Ground Elapsed Time.
The Flight Control team in Mission Control has elected not to do a GET clock update from 191 hours to 212 hours 48 minutes at this time.
Not doing this update at this time, we assure continuity and data being processed in the Mission Control Center from the last clock update through TEI.
The computers in Mission Control sit and work from a common time.
This common time [is] midnight the night before launch.

This update, if we chose to do it at this time, could conceivably have the effect of making the computers think that we launched Apollo 16 before that time if all elements of the MCC were thoroughly attuned to the update.
We were not sure that we had enough time to do that, so, our flight control team elected to be conservative and leave the option open for a clock update later.
We're at 190 hours 59 minutes [190:47] and standing by now for acquisition with Apollo 16.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Would you bring up the High Gain, please?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Could you give us High Gain?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, 191 hours 5 minutes [190:53] Ground Elapsed Time and we're receiving data now from Apollo 16.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Would you like to finish the updates?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I think I need the Updates Book.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: In the form of review, Ken, when we get to 192 hours, we're going to flip over to what was originally 214:00 in the Flight Plan, and that's going to become 192:10 [191:58].
And, at 192:10 [191:58], we work through those changes down to the point where I read you "Start hatch closing" at 194:23 [194:11].
And, at 194:27 [194:15], we want to jump back to the "LM jett procedures," and there is only one change in that.
So, I think that's the best place to go, which was at 176:50, originally.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You want to go at 194:27 [194:15] - is when you want to go the "LM jettison procedures?"
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, that's at 176:50 in the original Flight Plan, and 176:50 becomes 194:27 [194:15].
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; that means that all these things now are gonna be off by this screwy number.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Until we get - That makes LM jett come out on time by going to this screwy number here, 194:27 [194:15].
And, we pick up there, and we don't ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; I have crossed out 176:50, and I've called it 194:27 [194:15].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We delete the "VHF AM" and the "Configure the camera" business, and we pick up with the hatch closing.
Now, you can start those things early.
There is a - a little more time in here than was originally in the Flight Plan.
And you don't have to do the ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're going to need a little more with those suit donnings.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay; and the Direct O2Valve closed, you can delete that.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Now, other than changing your time columns like, turning the page there, the 177 there becomes 194:37 [194:25].
Everything is good through the sep maneuver.
The only change is that at 194:49 [194:37], the Noun 81, make ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Let me get the time column changed.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Apollo Control, Houston; [at] 191 hours, 10 minutes [190:58] Ground Elapsed Time, our Flight Director in the Control Center today is Phil Shaffer, relieving Gene Kranz on this shift.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: I got the time changed on the page.
Now you can read me some times.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay; 194:49, "The Noun 81 becomes minus 2.0 and 00." The CSM sep[aration] is at 2-foot-per-second retrograde.
This is so we can get the CSM out in front of the LM for TEI.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Is the LM not going to impact before we do TEI?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I think the planning is now for about 3 hours after TEI.
What they need to do is wait for the 210 antenna to come up so they can get data ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're not even going to get pictures of it, huh?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: after "Roll (4), off," - that's the completion of the little block following the sep, you can insert a remark "Doff PGA's," and at this time, we jump back over to what was ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, that's in the block after P00, after CSM sep 195:17 [195:05].
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: 195:17 [195:05] - I have as the first number on the top of the page.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay; 195:18 [195:06], after the completion of that little block, - that ends with "Roll (4), off," enter a little comment, "Doff PGAs," and go to Page 327.
That was originally 217 hours in the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: If I ever find my way through here.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Now, we want to do all these steps the that are down here at 195:20 or so, don't we?
It says doff PGAs there, but we really want to do all these things do so in that group, right?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I tried to dry them out.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: There's a towel that's right over your left shoulder, if you need it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, I guess I meant for you to do everything there except doffing the biomed harness, and the - of course, delete the uplinks and the other stuff..
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And 217 hours becomes 195:10 [194:58], and that's the time bias from then on, if you want to start updating your times.
And, where we come back in there is at the place - place where were supposed to uplink the vector.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And then we follow the rest of the flight plan All the way out?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, we pick up there at 195:18 and then from then on, we just follow sequentially - with all those little ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Why is it that there is no up-link in that?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, I've been advised that there's another Flight Plan change coming that's going to move the subsat blocks back a little bit, and to get in some mapping camera stuff.
Okay; I got it straight now.
We will not track the subsat.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You will not track the subsatellite.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: They're going to run the cameras to try to use the film.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: How about doing one thing for us, then?

If this is going to be the basic time line, and we're going to use these pages, is it reasonable to get you to give us a clock sync somewhere - so we - I'm afraid if I keep scratch - scratching out times and all in here, I'm going to miss something important.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, Ken, to fill you in on a little bit here, we originally planned a clock sync and that's why some of the confusion this morning.

Then we found out that it wouldn't work, because it put the lift-off time prior to time of midnight and wasn't sure it would work, and we couldn't find out in time enough before we had to start giving you this up through the LM jett, so we're going to work on that possibility.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: But for the time being, let's - let's just take one plan and run with it, and that is to update the times on every page.

transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston 192 hours [191:48] Ground Elapsed Time.
Mattingly will shortly be maneuvering Apollo 16 to a LM Comm attitude.

tokens: And Hydrogen Tank Heaters 1 and 2 in Auto.

transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, what we'd like to do, is get Canister 13 to the LM in place of the one we're bringing home.

tokens: I'm sorry, I still haven't understood you.

tokens: I packed all that stuff yesterday sometime, and it's over in the LM in a jett bag, and if we have extra canisters, I'd just as soon leave it that way if we could.

tokens: We're having enough trouble staying up the time line, without digging through a jett bag looking for a canister.

tokens: We don't want to do that.

tokens: You got the one that stuck last night.

transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay; and Orion's back on internal power at 192:06.

tokens: At 192 hours, 13 minutes [192:01] Ground Elapsed Time.
We've had Loss of Signal with Apollo 16.
As the spacecraft and crew pass over the backside of the Moon on the 60th revolution.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston at 192 hours 58 minutes [192:46] Ground Elapsed Time.
We're less than a minute away now from reacquiring Apollo 16.
We'll leave the line open, standby and monitor.
We are now receiving data from Apollo 16.
Apollo 16 now on its 61st revolution around the Moon.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Well, we're waiting for some LM - stuff, I guess.

tokens: The Noun 20s were for the Command Module: 33294, 11631, 30499.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're sitting in Hi bit rate, we've got Data and good signal strength.

transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And they are not quite ready with those up-links.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It was discussed, and the decision was made to power down.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And would you by chance have a crew status report?

transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, it's okay to leave the umbilicals hooked up.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We'd like to verify once again the docking angle.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We'll read it one more time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: What the problem is there, John, is we're still getting a torquing angle required, and we're having a little trouble with it.
And we're just trying to figure out what's wrong.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You want to check our arithmetic?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We did another Noun 20, so we got a problem with the X - X-axis.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Charlie, I got a new set of torquing angles for you.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we'd - after you've torqued that, would you get us another set of Noun 20s on the DSKY?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I copy minus 02900, minus 0820 - minus 08320, minus 04930.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Apollo Control, Houston, at 193 hours, 26 minutes [193:14] Ground Elapsed Time.
The Mission Control Center working with the crew of Apollo 16 as they go through their LM activation procedures.
Shortly, they should be donning their suits and preparation for hatch closure and LM jettison.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You have the Noun 20s on both DSKYs at 193:26:35.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Casper, I have a LM jettison PAD.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And if you'd flip the page, I'll give your sep time.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Casper, don't do this Verb 49 until we tell you to, please.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We need to keep the LM comm up is the reason.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And if you'd flip the page again, I'll give you your subsat jett PAD.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Can you give me some of these photo PADs so I can get them going, because this time after jettison is going to be pretty crowded, too?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'd like to get those down right now, before we get all suited up and ready to go.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll try to have them for you in a couple of minutes.
And, for Orion, I have the LM deorbit PAD.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, he'll be with you in just a second, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Speak to us, Henry, with the PADs.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: LM deorbit, 203:08:09.50 [202:56:19.50]; minus 0238.9, minus 0103.1, plus 0254.5; NA down to FDAI inertial, 197, 023; the rest is NA; LM weight, 5275.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: What'd you do with the pencil I gave you?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Start with - I got the Delta-Vs, but everything after that I missed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: [Laughter] That ought to tell them something about the changes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The rest is NA; LM weight, 5275.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And on the Noun 33, the seconds is 09.50.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Is that running out of ink tell you something about the changes we've had, Ken, I mean, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, but don't get behind the power curve because this cooling loop down here ain't got much.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ken, which PAD is it that you want?
Those on page 324 don't - don't apply.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You mentioned something about we're gonna get a whole lot of Mapping and Pan Camera stuff in there in those revs between there and TEI.
And I just thought that, if we had some idea of what's coming, we could do a better job of getting ready for it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This all occurs on the rev after next.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'd - I'd like to be able to plan a little further ahead.
If you don't get ahead - plan ahead, you just run a real good chance of not getting all the things done the way you'd like to.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And I'll try to have some words for you here in a few minutes.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, 16, for your information, LOS is at 194:11.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Orion, all your loads are in.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Can we start to maneuver to the jett attitude?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'd - we'd like for you to start on the callouts that are at 193:46 [193:34].
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We want look at data on telemetry before we start to maneuver because, when we do that, we go into Low Bit Rate and an Omni.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, the Mass Spec Ion Source is Off.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, the Alpha and X-ray covers are coming closed.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I haven't got time right now, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: in that setup, we forgot to get things configured for auto transfer protection on the glycol system there.
So I've got a short read-out here to make sure that's configured.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: On Panel 11, and I think you're already there - you checked, you got Pump 1, Pump 2, and the Auto Transfer breakers closed.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Open the Auto Transfer circuit breaker and then switch back to Pump 1.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, lastly, Charlie, close the Auto Transfer breaker, and we're back in business.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Casper, we're ready for Mass Spec, Retract.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The mass spec is coming now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Did you load your DAP before you started the P30?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We need Auto on the High Gain, Casper.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, our plan for the - for the camera work is, at about 196:45 [196:33], somewhere in there, it's sunrise.
We plan to run the Mapping Camera/Laser Altimeter throughout the - the daylight part of that Rev, up to about 197:50 [197:38].
And we'll bring the Pan Camera on at about - oh, a third of the way through that daylight portion, about 197:15 [197:03], and run it until darkness.
And we'll do a Solar Corona just prior to that, and maybe some handheld photography, which we'll read up to you after you doff your suits on the next rev.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Ken, it looks like the Mass Spec is jammed.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: 193:42:18 Comms Technician:Madrid comm tech, Houston comm tech, Net 1 voice check.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We seem to be out with Madrid.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We'll just stand by until they get through with it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We had a little trouble with the network there.
It appears that the Mass Spec boom is jammed, and we'd like for you to give it a 15-second Deploy and then back to Retract.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: How far did it look like it went before it jammed?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: About a third of the way in.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: There's 15 seconds, and I'm going to Retract.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Charlie has a visual on it, and it stopped again.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I've got the switch in the Off position now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, would you give us one more of those 15-second Deploy and back to Retract?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: How about if I just take it all the way out?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Why don't you go ahead and try it?
We have data shows it's stalling both ways.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Orion, we'd like you to verify Forward Omni before you secure the LM.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It - it didn't go all the way out and Charlie can visually verify that it is not all the way out.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Houston, can we start the new maneuver to the jett attitude?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Give us the SIM bay roll-jet configuration and start your Verb 49.
I think that's what you've got in there now.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And you are No Go for coupled jets.
I think we're going to have to jettison that boom.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Is that a requirement for - the LM jettison, you mean?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'll get you an answer to that.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're gonna keep the boom for LM jett, but keep the SIM bay jet configuration, so we don't go coupled.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: If we stay in the SIM bay jet configuration, what's this gonna do to our translation capability for the maneuver?
We gonna have to line it all up in one axis?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Minus X is what we want to do on that sep maneuver, anyhow.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: But that's probably not going to line up with the - with the attitude I'm unlocking in, is it?
Normally that turns out to be a three-axis burn.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Maneuver to get it all in minus-X, and then do it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: So you want me to maneuver to the minus-X attitude first.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We want to get the sep maneuver all in X, and I've got FDO trying to get you an attitude for that.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Well, I can probably figure that out from Noun 41, I mean, P41.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, FDO says the maneuver attitude to get that minus-X thruster is pitch, 327; yaw, 0; and roll, whatever you have.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: That'll be whatever roll we have, pitch of 327, and a yaw of 0.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we'd like to remind you we will need Omni Delta for AOS next time and to change the "verify" to a "configure the DSE after LOS to Low Bit Rate; Record; Forward."
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You're looking good at LOS, and AOS will be at 194:57 [194:45].
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, to reiterate: as far as that boom is concerned, it's the roll jets we're concerned with.
They're the only ones we have to keep single jet on.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Ken is off comm; he's suiting up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, John, would you - would you tell Ken that on that boom out, our only concern is going coupled on the roll.
So when he gets ready to do this burn, if he just wants to use X and Z and just leave his Y-translation - you know, make a 90-degree roll and burn a Z.
Now, Ken can handle it any way he wants, but just let him know our only concern is coupled jets in roll.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: No recording for this backside pass.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston; at 194 hours, 12 minutes [194:00] Ground Elapsed Time.
We've had a Loss Of Signal with Apollo 16.
As the spacecraft passes over the backside of the Moon on its 61st revolution the recalcitrant boom referred to as the Mass Spectrometer boom.
This item [is] approximately 25 feet in length from its stowed position.
It is formed by tempered steel tape.
It is electrically connected to the SIM bay via [a] cable at deployment.
If the boom cannot be retracted due to malfunction the boom activating mechanism, boom and experiment can be jettisoned.
Its location in the Service Module is in close proximity to the point of subsatellite deployment so we would not want the boom extended at the time of subsatellite deployment.
Also because of the limited mechanical strains of the boom, the boom must be retracted before any Service Propulsion System firings.
I repeat, if the boom cannot be retracted due to malfunction the boom activating mechanism, boom and experiment can be jettisoned.
We're at 194 hours, 14 minutes [194:02] Ground Elapsed Time and this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 194 hours, 19 minutes [194:07] Ground Elapsed Time.
A new data [sic] from our experiments controller indicates that we will be able to launch the subsatellite with [the] boom extended, it does not represent a constraint.

The only constraint we're presently faced is the burning of the Service Propulsion System engine so our cutoff time for retraction or jettison will be the Trans-Earth Injection burn when the Service Propulsion System engine would be used.
We're at 194 hours, 20 minutes [194:08] Ground Elapsed Time and this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO
